notes: Whether Scripture is interpreted allegorically or spiritually beyond the literal sense, recognizing multiple layers of meaning.
Whether the apostle Bartholomew carried the gospel to India and left a Hebrew Gospel of Matthew.
Whether the apostle Thomas carried the gospel to India as narrated in the Acts of Thomas.
Whether episcopal succession from the apostles is an explicit teaching.
Whether Christian communities were established in the Arabian Peninsula (Himyar, Najran).

Whether baptized Christians are called to a consecrated ascetic life as members of a covenant (bnay qyama / bnat qyama); a distinctive feature of early Syriac Christianity attested in Aphrahat and the Acts of Thomas.
Whether frequent eucharistic assembly is normative.
Whether baptism is explicitly described as illumination, enlightenment, or the bringing of divine light to the soul.
Mode and matter of baptism.
Whether baptism effects spiritual rebirth or regeneration.
Whether a post-baptismal anointing with chrism/oil is described as part of the baptismal rite.
Whether baptism is explicitly administered in the Trinitarian name of Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.
Whether valid sacraments require episcopal authorization.
Whether a threefold ministry of bishop, presbyter, and deacon is required.
Whether the literal resurrection of the same physical body at the last day is explicitly affirmed.
Whether Christianity reached the British Isles before the end of the Roman period.
Whether persons are called to follow Jesus.
Whether a formal period of pre-baptismal instruction (catechumenate) is required before baptism.
Whether virginity and celibacy are explicitly honored as a positive Christian calling, distinct from rejection of marriage.
Whether a text presents a future final judgment.
Whether prayer is described as a continual, unceasing spiritual practice extending throughout all of life.
Whether sexual continence, abstinence from meat, and/or abstinence from wine are required of all Christians; associated with Tatian, the Acts of Thomas, and some Syriac traditions.
Whether the bishop's chair/see, passed on in succession, is the locus of the apostolic faith.
Whether the unity of the Church is grounded in the collegial unity of bishops who each hold the whole episcopate in solidum.
Whether the Son is eternally begotten or generated from the Father — not created in time but proceeding from the Father from all eternity.
Whether Christianity was established in the Aksumite kingdom (Ethiopia/Eritrea) through mission and royal conversion.
Whether the Eucharist is described as conferring immortality or incorruption upon recipients.
Whether the Eucharist is identified as fulfilling Malachi 1:11's prophecy of a pure offering in every place.
Whether the Eucharist is explicitly described as a sacrifice or oblation.
Whether specific days of the week are mandated as Christian fast days.
Whether fasting is required as a Christian discipline.
Whether humans possess genuine free will as the basis for moral responsibility and divine judgment.
Whether Gentile believers are required to be circumcised for full inclusion.
Whether a harmonized gospel narrative (especially Tatian's Diatessaron) serves as the authoritative gospel text rather than four separate canonical gospels.
Whether the gospel reached India through apostolic or early missionary activity.
Whether baptism of infants and children is affirmed or presupposed.
Whether Jesus is confessed as the Christ.
Whether Jesus is proclaimed as having been raised from the dead.
Whether the kingdom of God is presented as having drawn near.
Whether Christians who lapsed during persecution (sacrificati, thurificati, libellatici) may be readmitted to communion after penance — and under what conditions.
Whether Christ is explicitly identified as the pre-existent Logos/Word of God through whom all things were created.
Whether martyrdom is explicitly presented as bearing witness to Christ and conforming to his passion.
Whether Christians are forbidden from serving in the military due to idolatry, bloodshed, and incompatibility with faith.
Whether a single bishop holds sole authority over the local church.
Whether remarriage after the death of a spouse is explicitly forbidden as incompatible with Christian purity.

dimension_label: Ordination by laying on of hands
notes: Whether ordination to ministry (especially episcopal ordination) is conferred by the laying on of hands by existing bishops.
Whether Mary remained a virgin not only in conceiving Jesus but perpetually — before, during, and after his birth.

dimension_label: Philosophy as schoolmaster for the Gospel
notes: Whether Greek philosophy is presented as a divinely permitted preparatory schoolmaster leading the Greeks toward the Gospel.
Whether post-baptismal sin can be forgiven through a second repentance.
Whether Christians are instructed to abstain from public games, theater, and spectacles as morally corrupting.
Whether eucharist is identified as Christ's body and blood.
Whether baptism administered by heretics or schismatics is valid — or must converts be rebaptized upon entering the Catholic Church.
Whether bodily remains of martyrs are treated as precious and gathered for commemorative celebration.
Whether repentance is required in response to the proclamation.
Whether a received rule of faith defines orthodox teaching.
Whether human souls pre-exist before their embodiment — existing in a prior spiritual state before being joined to bodies.
Whether Sunday is the regular day for eucharistic gathering.
Whether Jesus is portrayed as teaching with unique authority.
Whether God is explicitly described as three persons (personae) in one substance (substantia) — Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.
Whether Christ truly became flesh and truly suffered in the body; anti-docetist.
Whether all rational beings — including the damned and possibly demons — will eventually be restored to communion with God at the end of all things.
Whether Jesus was truly born of a virgin through the Holy Spirit.
